title: Boca Oriental Lighthouse and Ferry near Rio Grande Tierra del Fuego Coastal Border Crossing
description: Rio Grande, Tierra del Fuego: A pristine white lighthouse with striking red-and-white accents stands firmly against the vast, cloud-dappled expanse of the South Atlantic sky. The structure, known as Boca Oriental, commands the rugged coastline where concrete foundations meet weathered, broken rock and the deep blue churn of the sea. Sunlight bathes the scene in crisp, midday clarity, accentuating the sharp architectural contrast between the smooth paint of the lighthouse and the textured, jagged debris along the shoreline. In the distance, a ferry cuts a deliberate path across the horizon, serving as a vital link between the disparate regions of the southern archipelago. The composition utilizes a wide aperture to capture the interplay of the expansive sky and the grounded, utilitarian nature of this maritime navigational aid. Two birds glide gracefully overhead, adding a sense of movement to the otherwise static, stoic guard of the coast. This remote beacon symbolizes the navigational heritage and logistical connectivity required to traverse the extreme southern limits of the American continent. By documenting such transition points, one gains a deeper appreciation for the isolation and the resilience inherent in Patagonian maritime travel. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
